    Dental Services

    ​Restoring Smiles

    and Ensuring Oral Health 

    Schedule An Appointment

    Dental Implants

    Best replacement option for your missing teeth

    Dental Implant Crown
    Dental Implant Bridge
    Dental Implant Supported Denture

    Dental Implant Bridge

    Dental implants can also replace missing long edentulous spans by fabricating an implant supported dental bridge. 

    Dental Implant Supported Denture

    Dental implants can also be used to fabricate full arch denture to replace the whole set of teeth on the arch. 

    Dental Implant Crown

    Single implant retaining a crown on top. Dental Implants are proven to be the best way to replace the missing teeth function and aesthetics. At our office, we provide the highest grade of dental implants.

    Sure Smile | Clear Aligner Treatment 

    Non-intrusive and no braces needed to get your teeth straight. 

    Sure Smile

    Sure Smile

    Clear aligners are used to align your teeth in an optimal manner to achieve more function and cosmetically pleasing smiles. 

    You can book a non-obligation consultation to see what your smile would look like before and after Clear aligner treatment. 

    We always have special promotions going on our Sure smile aligner treatment, call us and book an appointment to know what suits you best. 

    Dental Prosthetics

    Crown & Bridge Work

    Dental Crown
    Dental Bridge
    Post & Core Build Up
    Dental Veneer
    Onlay/Inlay
    Removable Denture

    Post & Core Build Up

    This is an internal retention mechanism and  core filling required over a tooth that does not have enough tooth structure to receive crown coverage. Your dentist decides in your best interest, if you require this or not.  Pre-requisite for Post and core is  if the tooth has RCT done or would need one prior.

    Onlay/Inlay

    Not every tooth that has lost significant tooth structure to cavity or had RCT needs a crown, some can be dealt with a conservative option like inlay or an onlay. Your dentist best decides if the tooth would need a crown or an Onlay/Inlay.

    Dental Veneer

    Veneer are laminates customized  and fabricated to meet the cosmetic/esthetic needs. Veneers are used when a patient is looking for more aligned dentition, brighter and shiner smile, patients who are not happy with teeth shade even after multiple rounds of teeth whitening.

    Dental Bridge

    When there are 1 or 2 missing teeth in the dental arch, dental bridge is an option to replace the missing tooth/teeth. The bridge sits on the present teeth(retainers) and a unit of bridge called pontic replaces the missing tooth/teeth.

    Removable Denture

    Partial or a full plate that replaces the missing teeth to achieve the function of chewing and restoring the appearance of the patient.

    Dental Crown

    A crown or a cap is a term used for a customized lab-fabricated replacement of tooth structure. A crown is indicated when the tooth has lost a significant amount of structure due to decay or has a fracture line caused by stressful chewing or grinding habits. A crown serves two main functions: protection against heavy chewing forces and sealing the tooth to prevent acid attacks from the drinks and foods we consume.

    Dental Endodontics

    Root Canal Treatment

    Root Canal Treatment
    Post & Core Build Up
    Endo-Retreatment

    Endo-Retreatment

    When the initial root canal treatment has failed because of incomplete biomechanical canal preparation or persistent microorganism in the canal, an Endo retreatment is warranted to save the tooth. This procedure is generally done by an endodontist or general dentist with this skill set. 

    Post & Core Build Up

    This is an internal retention mechanism and  core filling required over a tooth that does not have enough tooth structure to receive crown coverage. Your dentist decides in your best interest, if you require this or not.  Pre-requisite for Post and core is  if the tooth has RCT done or would need one prior.

    Root Canal Treatment 

    Root canal treatment is a dental procedure to treat an infected tooth nerve. It involves removing the nerve, preparing the canal, and sealing it with a filling. Due to the weakened state of the treated tooth, it is advisable to protect it from fractures by applying a dental crown.

    Preventative Dentistry

    Preventing Dental Issues

    Dental Hygiene
    Resin/Composite Filling
    Resin GIC Filling

    Resin GIC Filling

    This is white colored filling as well, used mainly to restore the caries lesion over root surface, very deep decay on tooth, tooth fractures over the gum gum line. Best about this type of filling is that it releases fluoride over time therefore helps in future caries prevention.

    Resin/Composite Filling

    These are white coloured filling done to restore the part of tooth damaged to caries, fracture or chipped tooth.

    Dental Hygiene

    It is a very important dental visit where you get your teeth cleaned by Ultrasonic and manual scalers.  The focus of the visit is on prevention of dental disease like caries, gingivitis,gum recession, periodontitis, necrotising gingivitis, aggressive periodontitis etc. In addition, teeth polishing and fluoride application is part of the visit. Teeth brushing techniques and flossing is discussed to reinforce the home oral care routine.

    Dental Endodontics

    Root Canal Treatment

    Root Canal Treatment
    Post & Core Build Up
    Endo-Retreatment

    Endo-Retreatment

    When the initial root canal treatment has failed because of incomplete biomechanical canal preparation or persistent microorganism in the canal, an Endo retreatment is warranted to save the tooth. This procedure is generally done by an endodontist or general dentist with this skill set. 

    Post & Core Build Up

    This is an internal retention mechanism and  core filling required over a tooth that does not have enough tooth structure to receive crown coverage. Your dentist decides in your best interest, if you require this or not.  Pre-requisite for Post and core is  if the tooth has RCT done or would need one prior.

    Root Canal Treatment 

    Root canal treatment is a dental procedure to treat an infected tooth nerve. It involves removing the nerve, preparing the canal, and sealing it with a filling. Due to the weakened state of the treated tooth, it is advisable to protect it from fractures by applying a dental crown.

    Preventative Dentistry

    Preventing Dental Issues

    Dental Hygiene
    Resin/Composite Filling
    Resin GIC Filling
    1 Year Happy Visit
    Oral Cancer Screening

    Resin GIC Filling

    This is white coloured filling as well, used mainly to restore the caries lesion over root surface, very deep decay on tooth, tooth fractures over the gum gum line. Best about this type of filling is that it releases fluoride over time therefore helps in future caries prevention.

    Resin/Composite Filling

    These are white coloured filling done to restore the part of tooth damaged to caries, fracture or chipped tooth.

    Dental Hygiene

    It is a very important dental visit where you get your teeth cleaned by Ultrasonic and manual scalers.  The focus of the visit is on prevention of dental disease like caries, gingivitis,gum recession, periodontitis, necrotising gingivitis, aggressive periodontitis etc. In addition, teeth polishing and fluoride application is part of the visit. Teeth brushing techniques and flossing is discussed to reinforce the home oral care routine.

    1 Year Happy Visit

    For patients 1 / 1+  year old,  the dentist checks their present teeth and parents learn about the baby's oral health and how to take care at  home . Generally, We recommend dental visits once a year  for children until 5 yrs and every 6 months dental hygiene visits every 6 months from there onwards.

    Oral Cancer Screening

    OCS is a part of your once a year complete dental examination. During the screening the dentist checks extra-oral, intra-oral soft tissues, tongue,cheeks etc’ for early-signs of oral cancer and if anything suspicious then the patient is referred to specialist for further evaluation and necessary treatment. At our clinic we utilize the  machine “velscope” that is an additional tool for screening Oral cancer.

    Cosmetic Dentistry 

    Smile Makeovers with Dentistry

    Dental Veneer
    Resin/Composite Filling
    Gem/Diamond on Tooth

    Gem/Diamond on Tooth

    Every patient has unique demands, some require an extra shine on their tooth. Therefore, we could put a diamond on your tooth to stand out of the crowd.

    Resin/Composite Filling

    These are white coloured filling done to restore the part of tooth damaged to caries, fracture or chipped tooth.

    Dental Veneer

    Custom laminates designed to enhance the cosmetic appearance of teeth. They are used to achieve a more aligned dentition and a brighter, shinier smile for patients dissatisfied with their tooth shade despite multiple whitening attempts.

    Oral Surgery

    Tooth Removal & Related Surgeries

    Extractions
    Tissue Biopsy
    Wisdom Tooth Extraction

    Wisdom Tooth Extraction

    Wisdom teeth, or third molars, can cause problems. They are hard to clean, prone to cavities, can damage nearby molars if they grow improperly, and may become impacted, causing cyst-like issues. It's important to have your wisdom teeth checked and possibly removed by a dentist.

    Tissue Biopsy

    A biopsy is a tissue sample sent to the lab for microscopic examination. If you have any oral growths, whether soft or hard tissue, it is important to have them assessed by your dentist. Biopsies are conducted to examine and eliminate potential oral pathological conditions.

    Extractions

    Taking a severely damaged tooth out, which could be a simple extraction with  forceps or it could involve surgically sectioning and bone troughing to get the infected, non restorable tooth out.

    Resin/Composite Filling

    These are white coloured filling done to restore the part of tooth damaged to caries, fracture or chipped tooth.
